landing.step3_desc: Earn MULTA points when your report is verified. Climb the leaderboard, unlock badges, and build your civic reputation. When we go live on blockchain, your points convert to real tokens.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Multando ("we", "us", "our") operates the multando.com platform and related services. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, share, and protect your personal information in accordance with Colombian Law 1581 of 2012 (Habeas Data) and its regulatory decrees.
privacy.section1_subtitle: We collect information you provide directly and information generated through your use of the platform.
privacy.section1_personal_content: When you create an account, we collect your name, email address, phone number, and password. If you register through a third-party service, we may receive your name and email from that provider.
privacy.section1_location_content: We collect precise location data when you submit a traffic infraction report. This includes GPS coordinates, street address, and city. Location data from photo EXIF metadata may also be used to verify report accuracy.
privacy.section1_evidence_content: When you submit a report, you upload photographic or video evidence of the traffic infraction. This evidence may contain vehicle license plates, public roadways, and surrounding areas. We process these images using AI to extract relevant information.
privacy.section1_device_content: We automatically collect device type, operating system, browser type, IP address, and app version. This helps us maintain security, debug issues, and improve the platform experience.
privacy.section1_wallet_content: If you use the platform's reward system, we store your MULTA token balance, transaction history, and custodial wallet information. If you connect an external Solana wallet, we store your public wallet address.
privacy.section2_subtitle: Your data enables us to operate the platform, process reports, and distribute rewards.
privacy.section2_item1: Process and verify traffic infraction reports you submit
privacy.section2_item2: Distribute MULTA token rewards for verified reports
privacy.section2_item3: Submit verified reports to city authorities and the SIMIT/RECORD system
privacy.section2_item4: Analyze infraction evidence using artificial intelligence
privacy.section2_item5: Communicate with you about your reports, rewards, and account
privacy.section2_item6: Improve platform features, detect fraud, and prevent abuse
privacy.section2_item7: Generate anonymized analytics for city authorities
privacy.section3_subtitle: Important information about the public nature of blockchain transactions.
privacy.section3_content1: MULTA token transactions are recorded on the Solana blockchain, which is a public and immutable ledger. Once a transaction is recorded on-chain, it cannot be modified or deleted. Your public wallet address and transaction history are visible to anyone on the blockchain.
privacy.section3_content2: For custodial wallets managed by Multando, private keys are encrypted using AES-256 encryption and stored securely. We never expose private keys, and access is protected by multi-layer security controls.
privacy.section3_content3: If you switch to Advanced Mode and connect your own Solana wallet, you are solely responsible for the security of your private keys. Multando does not have access to or store your external wallet's private keys.
privacy.section4_subtitle: We share your information only when necessary to operate the platform and fulfill its purpose.
privacy.section4_authorities_content: Verified traffic infraction reports, including evidence, location, and vehicle information, are shared with relevant municipal transit authorities for processing. Reports may be submitted to Colombia's RECORD system (Registro de Contravenciones y Multas).
privacy.section4_whatsapp_content: If you interact with our WhatsApp chatbot to submit reports, your phone number and messages are processed through the WhatsApp Business API operated by Meta Platforms, Inc.
privacy.section4_ai_content: Photos and videos you upload are processed by Anthropic's AI models to detect license plates, identify infraction types, and extract location data. Images are processed in transit and not retained by the AI provider beyond processing.
privacy.section4_storage_content: Evidence files (photos and videos) are stored securely on Amazon Web Services (AWS) S3 with server-side encryption. Data may be processed in AWS regions outside Colombia, in compliance with applicable data transfer regulations.
privacy.section4_captcha_content: When submitting reports to the Colombian government's RECORD system, we use third-party services (2Captcha) to complete the submission form on your behalf.
privacy.section5_subtitle: We retain your data for as long as necessary to provide our services.
privacy.section5_content1: Account information is retained for as long as your account is active. If you delete your account, we will remove your personal information within 30 days, except where retention is required by law.
privacy.section5_content2: Traffic infraction reports and evidence are retained for a minimum of 5 years, as required by Colombian transit regulations, even if your account is deleted.
privacy.section5_content3: Blockchain transactions are permanent and immutable. Token transaction records on the Solana blockchain cannot be deleted.
privacy.section5_content4: Server logs and analytics data are retained for up to 12 months for security and operational purposes.
privacy.section6_subtitle: Under Colombian Law 1581 of 2012, you have the following ARCO rights regarding your personal data.
privacy.section6_access_content: You have the right to know what personal data we hold about you and how it is being used. You can request a copy of your data at any time.
privacy.section6_rectification_content: You have the right to request correction of inaccurate or incomplete personal data. Update your profile directly or contact us.
privacy.section6_cancellation_content: You have the right to request deletion of your personal data, subject to legal retention requirements. Note that blockchain records cannot be deleted.
privacy.section6_opposition_content: You have the right to object to the processing of your personal data for certain purposes, such as marketing communications.
privacy.section6_how_to: To exercise any of these rights, email us at privacy@multando.com. We will respond within 15 business days as required by law.
privacy.section7_subtitle: We use cookies and similar technologies to improve your experience.
privacy.section7_content1: Essential cookies are required for the platform to function properly, including authentication, language preferences, and security tokens.
privacy.section7_content2: Analytics cookies help us understand how users interact with the platform so we can improve features and performance.
privacy.section7_content3: You can control cookie preferences through your browser settings. Disabling essential cookies may prevent certain platform features from working correctly.
privacy.section8_subtitle: We take the protection of your data seriously.
privacy.section8_content1: All data transmitted between your device and our servers is encrypted using TLS 1.3. Sensitive data at rest, including wallet private keys, is encrypted using AES-256.
privacy.section8_content2: We implement role-based access controls, audit logging, and regular security reviews. Our infrastructure is hosted on AWS with industry-standard security certifications.
privacy.section8_content3: While we implement robust security measures, no system is completely secure. We encourage you to use strong passwords and enable two-factor authentication on your account.
privacy.section9_subtitle: Our platform is not intended for users under 18 years of age.
privacy.section9_content: Multando does not knowingly collect personal information from children under 18. If you are a parent or guardian and believe your child has provided us with personal information, please contact us at privacy@multando.com and we will promptly delete such information.
privacy.section10_subtitle: We may update this policy from time to time.
privacy.section10_content: If we make material changes to this Privacy Policy, we will notify you by email and/or by posting a prominent notice on the platform at least 15 days before the changes take effect. Your continued use of the platform after the effective date constitutes your acceptance of the updated policy.
privacy.section11_subtitle: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy or your personal data.
privacy.section11_content: For privacy-related inquiries, data access requests, or to exercise your ARCO rights, please contact us:
privacy.section11_authority: You may also file a complaint with the Superintendencia de Industria y Comercio (SIC), Colombia's data protection authority.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: These Terms of Service ("Terms") govern your access to and use of the Multando platform, including our website, mobile applications, APIs, and related services (collectively, the "Platform"). By accessing or using the Platform, you agree to be bound by these Terms.
terms.section1_content: By creating an account, accessing, or using any part of the Platform, you confirm that you have read, understood, and agree to be bound by these Terms and our Privacy Policy. If you do not agree, you must not use the Platform.
terms.section2_content1: You must be at least 18 years of age to use the Platform. By registering, you represent and warrant that you are at least 18 years old and have the legal capacity to enter into these Terms.
terms.section2_content2: The Platform is currently available in Colombia. Certain features, including government report submission and token rewards, may be limited to users physically located within Colombia.
terms.section3_content1: You must provide accurate, complete, and current information when creating your account. You are responsible for maintaining the accuracy of this information.
terms.section3_content2: You may only create one account per person. Creating multiple accounts to manipulate rewards, verification, or any other platform feature is strictly prohibited and will result in permanent suspension.
terms.section3_content3: You are responsible for maintaining the security of your account credentials. You must notify us immediately at security@multando.com if you suspect unauthorized access to your account.
terms.section4_content1: All traffic infraction reports must be truthful, accurate, and based on events you personally witnessed. You must provide genuine photographic or video evidence captured at the time and location of the infraction.
terms.section4_content2: Submitting false, fabricated, or misleading reports is strictly prohibited. Infractions include staging incidents, using manipulated images, reporting vehicles that did not commit an infraction, and resubmitting previously rejected reports without new evidence.
terms.section4_content3: Consequences for false reporting include temporary or permanent account suspension, forfeiture of all earned MULTA tokens, removal from leaderboards and achievement systems, and potential legal action under Colombian law.
terms.section4_content4: You must not endanger yourself or others when capturing evidence. Never operate a vehicle while using the platform. Always prioritize personal safety over capturing evidence.
terms.section5_content1: The Platform uses a community-based verification system where users review and vote on reports submitted by others. By participating in verification, you agree to review reports honestly and in good faith.
terms.section5_content2: You must not collude with other users to verify false reports, use multiple accounts to influence verification outcomes, or deliberately reject valid reports. Such behavior constitutes platform manipulation and will result in suspension.
terms.section5_content3: Verification decisions are advisory. Multando and relevant city authorities retain final authority over whether a report leads to an official traffic citation.
terms.section6_content1: MULTA tokens are digital reward tokens on the Solana blockchain. They are earned by submitting verified traffic infraction reports and participating in community verification. MULTA tokens are not a security, investment product, or currency.
terms.section6_content2: Multando makes no guarantees regarding the monetary value of MULTA tokens. Token value may fluctuate and may be zero. You should not acquire or hold tokens with an expectation of profit.
terms.section6_content3: You may stake MULTA tokens for additional rewards at rates determined by the platform. Staking terms, including APY rates and lock-up periods, may change with 30 days' notice. Staked tokens may be subject to an unstaking cooldown period.
terms.section6_content4: Withdrawals to external Solana wallets are subject to daily and monthly limits, processing times, and network fees. Multando reserves the right to delay or refuse withdrawals if fraud or policy infractions are suspected.
terms.section7_content1: By default, Multando creates and manages a custodial wallet on your behalf. Private keys for custodial wallets are encrypted and stored securely. You may access your tokens through the Platform without any blockchain knowledge.
terms.section7_content2: Multando is not liable for changes in the market value of MULTA tokens held in your custodial wallet. We do not provide financial advice, and tokens should not be treated as a financial investment.
terms.section7_content3: You may switch to Advanced Mode at any time to connect your own Solana wallet. In Advanced Mode, you are solely responsible for the security of your wallet and private keys. Multando cannot recover lost external wallet keys.
terms.section8_content1: Developers may access the Platform through our APIs and SDKs subject to separate API terms. API keys are personal and non-transferable. You must keep your API keys confidential.
terms.section8_content2: API usage is subject to rate limits: 60 requests per minute for production, 300 for sandbox. Exceeding rate limits may result in temporary throttling or key revocation.
terms.section8_content3: Automated scraping, data harvesting, or systematic downloading of Platform content without authorization is prohibited. All integrations must provide appropriate attribution to Multando.
terms.section9_subtitle: The following activities are strictly prohibited on the Platform:
terms.section9_item1: Submitting false, staged, or manipulated traffic infraction reports
terms.section9_item2: Harassing, threatening, or intimidating other users, vehicle owners, or authorities
terms.section9_item3: Manipulating the verification system, rewards, or leaderboards
terms.section9_item4: Attempting to hack, reverse-engineer, or exploit vulnerabilities in the Platform
terms.section9_item5: Creating multiple accounts or using bots to inflate rewards
terms.section9_item6: Using the Platform to stalk, dox, or track specific individuals or vehicles
terms.section9_item7: Sharing or selling account credentials or API keys
terms.section9_item8: Interfering with the operation of the Platform or its infrastructure
terms.section10_content1: The Multando platform, including its design, code, brand, logos, and documentation, is owned by Multando S.A.S. and protected by Colombian and international intellectual property laws. You may not copy, modify, or distribute any part of the Platform without written permission.
terms.section10_content2: You retain ownership of the photos and videos you submit as evidence. By uploading content, you grant Multando a non-exclusive, worldwide, royalty-free license to use, process, store, and share your evidence for the purposes of operating the Platform, including sharing with authorities.
terms.section11_content1: The Platform is provided "as is" and "as available" without warranties of any kind, whether express or implied. Multando does not guarantee uninterrupted access, accuracy of AI analysis, or that all reports will result in official citations.
terms.section11_content2: To the maximum extent permitted by Colombian law, Multando shall not be liable for indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages arising from your use of the Platform, including but not limited to loss of tokens, loss of data, or third-party claims.
terms.section11_content3: Multando's total aggregate liability for any claim arising from or related to the Platform shall not exceed the value of MULTA tokens earned by you in the 12 months preceding the claim.
terms.section12_content1: These Terms are governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Republic of Colombia. Any dispute arising from or related to these Terms or the Platform shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Bogota, Colombia.
terms.section12_content2: Before initiating any legal proceeding, both parties agree to attempt resolution through good-faith negotiation for a period of 30 calendar days.
terms.section13_content1: You may delete your account at any time through your profile settings. Upon deletion, your personal data will be removed in accordance with our Privacy Policy, subject to legal retention requirements.
terms.section13_content2: Multando reserves the right to suspend or terminate your account immediately, without prior notice, if you violate these Terms, submit false reports, or engage in prohibited conduct. Upon termination for infractions, any earned MULTA tokens may be forfeited.
terms.section13_content3: Sections relating to intellectual property, limitation of liability, governing law, and blockchain data survive termination of these Terms.
terms.section14_content: For questions about these Terms of Service, please contact us:

rewards_info.banner_desc: Your MULTA points are currently tracked in our secure database. We're running a parallel test token on Solana's devnet (a test network with no real value). When Multando launches across multiple cities, your accumulated points will convert to real MULTA tokens on the Solana blockchain — tradeable, stakeable, and fully yours.
rewards_info.devnet_explanation: It's Solana's test network where developers build and test without real money. It proves our blockchain infrastructure works and is ready for mainnet launch.
rewards_info.staking_preview_desc: Test how staking will work when we go live on blockchain. Your staked points earn bonus rewards at the same 5% APY rate that will apply to real tokens.
rewards_info.points_note: MULTA points have no monetary value at this time. They represent your contribution to road safety and will be the basis for future token distribution.
rewards_info.learn_more: Learn more about our blockchain roadmap

footer.tagline: Report traffic infractions, earn rewards, and help make roads safer for everyone in your community.

about.hero_subtitle: A citizen-powered platform that turns everyday reports into safer streets and real rewards.
about.mission_p1: Multando is a civic tech platform built to tackle Colombia's traffic infraction crisis. We give every citizen a simple, secure way to report dangerous driving and unsafe road conditions.
about.mission_p2: By combining community reports with AI validation and blockchain-based rewards, we turn participation into meaningful change on the road.
about.story_p1: Multando started with a simple question: what if reporting a traffic infraction was as easy as snapping a photo, and what if doing the right thing came with real recognition?
about.story_p2: We built a platform where citizens, AI, and blockchain work together. Reports are verified automatically, validated by authorities when needed, and contributors earn MULTA rewards for every confirmed infraction.
about.story_p3: Today, we work alongside local authorities and open-source developers to make road safety a shared responsibility, backed by transparent technology.
about.tech_subtitle: We combine mobile, AI, and blockchain so every report is fast to file and easy to trust.
about.tech_mobile_content: A polished web and mobile experience, plus SDKs for partners to integrate reporting into their own apps.
about.tech_ai_content: Vision models check photo evidence, detect license plates, and flag low-quality reports before they reach authorities.
about.tech_blockchain_content: MULTA rewards run on Solana so contributions are transparent, auditable, and belong to the people who earn them.
about.tech_geo_content: Every report is tagged with precise location data, powering heat maps and helping cities act where it matters.
about.cta_subtitle: Whether you are a city, a partner, or a curious citizen, we would love to hear from you.

contact.title: Contact Multando
contact.subtitle: We read every message. Tell us what you need and we will get back to you as soon as we can.
contact.form_subtitle: Fill in the form and your email client will open a pre-filled message to us.

cookies.title: Cookie Policy
cookies.intro: This page explains what cookies Multando uses, why we use them, and how you can control them.
cookies.what_content: Cookies are small text files stored on your device when you visit a website. They help the site remember your actions and preferences (such as login status and language) so you do not have to re-enter them each time you return.
cookies.types_subtitle: Only what is needed for Multando to work well
cookies.type_essential_content: Required for core features like authentication, session management, and security. Without them, you could not log in or submit reports safely.
cookies.type_analytics_content: Help us understand how people use Multando so we can improve the platform. Always aggregated, never tied to your identity.
cookies.type_prefs_content: Remember your choices, such as language and theme, so you have a consistent experience across visits.
cookies.disable_content: You can block or delete cookies at any time through your browser settings. Each browser is a bit different, but the controls are typically found here:
cookies.disable_warning: Heads up: blocking essential cookies will prevent you from logging in and using some features of Multando.
cookies.contact_subtitle: Get in touch about cookies or privacy
cookies.contact_content: If you have any questions about how we use cookies, reach out to our privacy team.

blog.title: Blog coming soon
blog.subtitle: We are putting together stories about road safety, civic tech, and the community behind Multando. Be the first to read when we publish.
blog.signup_notice: Coming soon - this signup is a placeholder while we build the newsletter.

careers.title: We are growing!
careers.subtitle: Multando is a small team on a mission to make roads safer across Latin America. We are always looking for people who care about civic impact and great product craft.
careers.no_positions_desc: We do not have active roles listed today, but if you love what we are building, we want to meet you.
careers.email_notice: Send us your CV, a link to your work, and a short note about why Multando.
careers.value_impact_content: Ship work that shows up in your city, not just on a roadmap.
careers.value_care_content: We sweat the details, from API contracts to map pins.
careers.value_remote_content: Work from anywhere in LATAM with flexible hours and a strong async culture.
